title: The moral of the story

The narrative body it is supposed to summarize — a coherent multi-sentence story — only stabilises at step 600. The closing template crystallises 200 steps before the body it summarises.

title: Narrative conflict

headline_example.generation: Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to write pictures and draw pictures. One day, she went to the park to play with her friends. They were having so much fun! But then, a big wind came by and everything started to rain. Lily was sad because she could not play outside. She said

before_example.generation: Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to write on her paper. She would write on her paper every day. One day, Lily found a big book on her paper. It was a picture of a butterfly. Lily read the picture and felt very excited. "Look at this pretty butterfly!" she said

explanation: At step 5000 stories develop internal tension. "But then, a big wind came by" — an event interrupts the happy setup, with consequence and emotion following. The model has narrative structure, not just sentence chaining.
tweet_text: Step 5000: stories acquire conflict. "But then, a big wind came by" — a counter-event sets up the arc.

title: Plural agreement

headline_example.generation: There were two bears, who were the best. They went to the beach together to make a new home. From that day on, the bears made sure the bears were comfortable, and they were the best of friends. The bears and bears were the happiest bears in the world.

before_example.generation: There were two The two friends looked at each other and smiled. They were happy. The two friends went back to the garden. They had lots of fun playing together, and they were both happy.

explanation: At step 7000 the slot after "There were two" is reliably filled with a plural noun. Two thousand steps earlier the slot was just as often a stalled sentence break or a stray quotation mark.
tweet_text: Step 7000: "There were two ___" becomes plural. Number agreement is learned.
